_comment: BatchMaster-Toolkit manifest — single source of truth for all scripts across platforms.

risk_levels.LOW: Reads data only. No files modified, no settings changed.
risk_levels.MEDIUM: Modifies cache or temp files. Changes are non-critical and easily reversible.
risk_levels.HIGH: Modifies system settings, registry, or service configuration. Reversible with included undo option.
risk_levels.CRITICAL: Permanently deletes files or makes irreversible system changes. Backup required before use.

System-Diagnostics.description: Full interactive analytics dashboard covering CPU, RAM, GPU, disk, battery, network, processes, startup programs, Windows license, and security — with live ASCII bar charts for CPU load and disk usage.
System-Diagnostics.long_description: 14-section interactive menu. Uses WGet/WGetFull subroutines to strip wmic carriage-return artifacts for accurate data. Disk sizes computed via 9-digit string trim to avoid 32-bit set /a overflow. All output constrained to 120 columns.

4.0.0.notes: Complete rewrite with WGet/WGetFull subroutines to fix wmic CR stripping. Added per-drive GB math, battery charge meter, and 120-col window scaling.

3.0.0.notes: Added ASCII bar charts for CPU load and disk usage.

2.0.0.notes: Added GPU, battery, and security sections.

System-Diagnostics.description: Detailed battery health report showing wear level percentage, full charge capacity vs design capacity, estimated cycle count, and a color-coded health alert when capacity drops below 80%.
System-Diagnostics.long_description: Queries Win32_Battery via wmic. Calculates wear as (1 - FullChargeCapacity/DesignCapacity)*100. Generates a full HTML battery report via powercfg /batteryreport saved to the Desktop.

System-Diagnostics.description: Lists all installed device drivers with version numbers, dates, and publisher. Flags drivers older than 12 months and any unsigned or unverified drivers.
System-Diagnostics.long_description: Uses wmic path win32_PnPSignedDriver to enumerate all drivers. Compares DriverDate against current date to identify stale drivers. Checks IsSigned field to flag unsigned drivers. Output sortable by date or name.

System-Diagnostics.description: Reads Windows event log ID 100 (boot performance) and startup impact entries. Ranks which startup programs add the most seconds to boot time with suggestions to disable top offenders.
System-Diagnostics.long_description: Queries the Microsoft-Windows-Diagnostics-Performance operational log via wevtutil. Parses startup delay data from event ID 100 fields. Also reads HKLM startup registry keys and measures impact against known delay thresholds.

System-Diagnostics.description: Live auto-refreshing loop reading CPU and disk temperatures via WMI MSAcpi_ThermalZoneTemperature. Color-coded warning when CPU exceeds 80°C.
System-Diagnostics.long_description: Polls wmic /namespace:\root\wmi PATH MSAcpi_ThermalZoneTemperature every 3 seconds. Converts raw decikelvin to Celsius. Warn threshold: 80°C (yellow), Critical threshold: 90°C (red). Compatible hardware required for WMI thermal support.

Maintenance-Cleaning.description: 14-step system deep clean: temp folders, prefetch, thumbnails, DNS cache, all browser caches, Windows Update cache, recycle bin, error reports, event logs, and silent Disk Cleanup.
Maintenance-Cleaning.long_description: Cleans: %TEMP%, C:\Windows\Temp, Prefetch, thumbcache_*.db, ipconfig /flushdns, IE/Edge WebCache, Edge/Chrome/Firefox code and GPU caches, SoftwareDistribution\Download, Recycle Bin, WER directories, all event logs via wevtutil, and cleanmgr /sagerun:1.

2.0.0.notes: Added Firefox and Edge cache paths. Added event log clearing and silent Disk Cleanup.

Maintenance-Cleaning.description: Clears Windows RAM standby list and forces working set trim. Displays before and after physical memory usage to measure actual freed RAM in MB.
Maintenance-Cleaning.long_description: Uses PowerShell [System.GC]::Collect() and optionally RAMMap.exe -Ew if available. Reads TotalVisibleMemorySize and FreePhysicalMemory via wmic before and after to calculate freed memory. Safe to run on any running system.

Maintenance-Cleaning.description: Scans all logical drives for files over 100 MB. Ranked list sorted by descending size with full path, size in MB/GB, and last modified date.
Maintenance-Cleaning.long_description: Uses forfiles /s /m *.* /c to recurse drives. Filters by size threshold. Converts bytes to human-readable MB/GB. Results sorted and displayed in a table. Output optionally saved to Desktop\LargeFiles_report.txt.

Maintenance-Cleaning.description: Recursively scans a folder for files sharing the same name and byte size. Lists all duplicate groups with full paths for manual review.
Maintenance-Cleaning.long_description: Prompts for root folder. Uses dir /s /b to enumerate all files. Groups by filename+size hash. Displays duplicate groups with their full paths. Does NOT auto-delete — user reviews and removes manually.

Maintenance-Cleaning.description: Shows recycle bin size per drive in MB and GB. Interactive menu to empty individual bins or all at once, with confirmation prompt before any deletion.
Maintenance-Cleaning.long_description: Reads $Recycle.Bin on each logical drive. Calculates size with dir /s. Interactive menu: (1) list bins with sizes, (2) empty selected drive, (3) empty all. Requires Y/N confirmation before any deletion.

Network-Tools.description: Full network tune-up: flush DNS, reset Winsock and TCP/IP stack, set fast DNS servers, disable throttling. Includes before/after ping latency comparison.
Network-Tools.long_description: Operations: ipconfig /flushdns, ipconfig /release + /renew, netsh winsock reset, netsh int ip reset, netsh int tcp set global (autotuninglevel, chimney, dca), registry key NetworkThrottlingIndex=0xFFFFFFFF, arp -d *, ipconfig /registerdns, DNS set to 8.8.8.8 + 1.1.1.1 on all active adapters. Includes undo option restoring DHCP DNS.

Network-Tools.description: Enumerates all saved WiFi profiles and displays each network SSID with its stored plaintext password from the local Windows credential store.
Network-Tools.long_description: Uses netsh wlan show profiles to list all saved SSIDs. For each SSID runs netsh wlan show profile name='...' key=clear and parses the Key Content field. No external tools. Works only on the local machine — cannot read passwords from other devices.

Network-Tools.description: Scans the 50 most common TCP/UDP ports on localhost. Reports open ports and maps each to its owning process name and PID.
Network-Tools.long_description: Uses netstat -ano to get listening sockets. For each open port, resolves process name via tasklist /fi PID. PowerShell Test-NetConnection used as fallback for UDP probing. Output table separates well-known ports (<1024) from ephemeral ports.

Network-Tools.description: Pings 5 servers every 10 seconds for 5 minutes. Appends timestamped results to a latency log on the Desktop for trend analysis.
Network-Tools.long_description: Targets: Google DNS (8.8.8.8), Cloudflare (1.1.1.1), OpenDNS (208.67.222.222), Quad9 (9.9.9.9), Amazon (205.251.196.1). Each cycle pings all 5 targets and logs: timestamp, target, min/avg/max RTT, and packet loss percentage. Total run time: 5 minutes (30 cycles).

Network-Tools.description: Lists all established TCP connections with remote IP, port, and process name. Select by number to immediately terminate the owning process.
Network-Tools.long_description: Uses netstat -ano | findstr ESTABLISHED. Resolves PIDs to process names via tasklist. Numbers each connection for selection. Terminates selected connection via taskkill /f /pid. Requires Y confirmation before kill. Refreshes list after termination.

Security-Privacy.description: Disables Windows telemetry services, Cortana tracking, advertising ID, WER, and CEIP via registry. Blocks tracking domains in hosts file. Fully reversible.
Security-Privacy.long_description: Disables: DiagTrack and dmwappushservice services, AllowTelemetry=0, DisableAdvertisingId=1, AllowCortana=0, DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications=1, WER disabled, CEIP opt-out. Appends ~30 known Microsoft telemetry endpoints to C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts. Option [2] fully restores all defaults.

Security-Privacy.description: Lists all local user accounts with last login timestamp, status, and password age. Flags unused accounts and those with blank or expired passwords.
Security-Privacy.long_description: Uses net user and wmic useraccount. Reads LastLogon, PasswordLastSet, AccountType, Disabled flags. Flags: never-logged-in accounts, accounts with PasswordRequired=False, accounts inactive for 90+ days, and built-in Administrator if enabled.

Security-Privacy.description: Scans 12 autorun registry keys and all startup folders. Flags entries pointing to temp directories, missing paths, or AppData locations used by malware.
Security-Privacy.long_description: Scans: HKCU/HKLM Run, RunOnce, RunServices, RunServicesOnce. Also scans %APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup and the All Users equivalent. For each entry: checks if path exists, flags paths in %TEMP%, %APPDATA%, Downloads. Displays full registry key path for manual removal.

Security-Privacy.description: Scans all running processes and flags any executing from %TEMP%, %APPDATA%, %LOCALAPPDATA%, Downloads, or other non-standard locations.
Security-Privacy.long_description: Iterates all processes via wmic process get Name,ExecutablePath,ProcessId. For each path checks against a suspicious-location list: Temp, AppData\Local, AppData\Roaming, Downloads, Desktop, Public. Also flags processes with no path (injected threads), processes masquerading as system names from wrong directories.

Utility-Scripts.description: Pre-gaming optimizer: kills background apps, sets High Performance power plan, disables update scanning, boosts network latency. Includes full Restore option.
Utility-Scripts.long_description: Boost: kills 13 background processes (OneDrive, Teams, Slack, Discord, Spotify, etc.), powercfg /setactive High-Performance GUID, stops wuauserv + bits, Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring $true, sets TcpAckFrequency=1 + TCPNoDelay=1, Win32PrioritySeparation=38. Restore: reverses all 6 changes with exact original values.

Utility-Scripts.description: Moves all files in a folder into typed subfolders by extension: Images, Documents, Videos, Music, Archives, Others.
Utility-Scripts.long_description: Extension mapping — Images: jpg,jpeg,png,gif,bmp,webp,svg,ico,tiff; Documents: pdf,doc,docx,xls,xlsx,ppt,pptx,txt,csv,md,odt; Videos: mp4,mkv,avi,mov,wmv,flv,m4v; Music: mp3,flac,wav,aac,ogg,wma,m4a; Archives: zip,rar,7z,tar,gz,bz2,xz. All other extensions go to Others. Shows count of files moved per category.

Utility-Scripts.description: Interactive backup: prompts for source and destination, creates a BACKUP_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS subfolder, copies recursively with retry logic and live progress counter.
Utility-Scripts.long_description: Uses robocopy /e /r:3 /w:5 /np /tee for reliable recursive copy with 3 retries and 5-second waits. Creates timestamped destination subfolder. Logs output to backup_log.txt alongside the backup. Prints file count and total size on completion.

Utility-Scripts.description: Silent bulk installer via winget. Checks if already installed before proceeding. Installs Chrome, VLC, 7-Zip, Notepad++, and VS Code. Logs results to Desktop.
Utility-Scripts.long_description: For each app: runs winget list --id <package_id> to check if installed. If not, runs winget install --id <package_id> --silent --accept-package-agreements --accept-source-agreements. Package IDs: Google.Chrome, VideoLAN.VLC, 7zip.7zip, Notepad++.Notepad++, Microsoft.VisualStudioCode. Results logged to Desktop\install_log.txt.

Utility-Scripts.description: 10 weighted diagnostic checks produce a health score out of 100 with a letter grade and specific improvement recommendations.
Utility-Scripts.long_description: Checks and weights: RAM usage free >50% (20pts), C: drive >20% free (20pts), CPU load <70% (15pts), battery >50% (10pts), Firewall enabled (10pts), Defender running (10pts), listening ports <20 (5pts), startup items <10 (5pts), no pending Windows Update (3pts), uptime <7 days (2pts). Grade: A=90+, B=80+, C=70+, D=60+, F=<60.

Utility-Scripts.description: Benchmarks CPU arithmetic, disk read/write, and RAM throughput. Results table with five-tier performance rating.
Utility-Scripts.long_description: CPU: timed loop counting iterations/second. Disk: fsutil creates 500 MB test file, measures write MB/s and read MB/s, then deletes test file. RAM: PowerShell array copy speed test. Tiers: Entry (<index 20), Mid-Range (20-40), Good (40-60), High-End (60-80), Enthusiast (80+). All temp files auto-deleted after test.

System-Diagnostics.description: Full system snapshot via system_profiler, sysctl, vm_stat, df, and ioreg. CPU, RAM, GPU, disk, battery, and uptime in a formatted terminal dashboard.
System-Diagnostics.long_description: Sources: system_profiler SPHardwareDataType (CPU/RAM/serial), sysctl hw.memsize + kern.version, vm_stat (page calculations for used/free RAM), df -h (disk), system_profiler SPDisplaysDataType (GPU), ioreg -rn AppleSmartBattery (battery/MacBook only). Uptime via sysctl kern.boottime.

System-Diagnostics.description: Reads battery cycle count, condition, max and design capacity from ioreg. Calculates wear % and flags batteries past 20% or 40% wear.
System-Diagnostics.long_description: Extracts from ioreg -rn AppleSmartBattery: CycleCount, Condition, AppleRawMaxCapacity, DesignCapacity. Wear% = (1 - MaxCapacity/DesignCapacity)*100. Thresholds: >20% = Degraded (yellow), >40% = Replace Soon (red). Apple's official threshold for service is 80% worn (i.e. 80% capacity remaining).

System-Diagnostics.description: Runs diskutil on all physical disks. Reports SMART status, partition scheme, file system, and capacity. If smartmontools installed, reads full SMART attribute table.
System-Diagnostics.long_description: diskutil list enumerates all physical disks. diskutil info /dev/diskN extracts: MediaType, SmartStatus, PartitionMapType, FilesystemType, MediaName, Size. If smartctl is available (brew install smartmontools), reads SMART attributes: ID 5 (reallocated sectors), ID 187 (uncorrectable errors), ID 194 (temperature), ID 9 (power-on hours).

Maintenance-Cleaning.description: Clears ~/Library/Caches, flushes DNS, rebuilds Spotlight index, runs Homebrew cleanup if installed, removes crash logs, purges memory, empties Trash.
Maintenance-Cleaning.long_description: Steps: rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/* (user cache), dscacheutil -flushcache + killall -HUP mDNSResponder (DNS), sudo mdutil -E / (Spotlight rebuild), brew cleanup --prune=all + autoremove (if brew installed), rm -rf ~/Library/Logs/*.crash (crash logs), sudo purge (RAM), osascript to empty Trash. Reports reclaimed space before and after.

Maintenance-Cleaning.description: Full Homebrew maintenance cycle: update, upgrade formulae and casks, cleanup --prune=all, autoremove unused deps, doctor. Reports reclaimed space.
Maintenance-Cleaning.long_description: Sequence: brew update (refresh index), brew upgrade (all formulae), brew upgrade --cask (all casks), brew cleanup --prune=all (remove all old versions and downloads), brew autoremove (unused leaf dependencies), brew doctor (configuration health). Measures df -h before and after to report reclaimed space.

Maintenance-Cleaning.description: Finds files over 500 MB on the full filesystem, excluding /System, /dev, /private/var/vm. Sorted list with human-readable sizes.
Maintenance-Cleaning.long_description: find / -size +500M -not -path '/System/*' -not -path '/dev/*' -not -path '/private/var/vm/*' -not -path '/Library/Developer/*' 2>/dev/null. Pipes to du -sh for human-readable sizes. Sorted by size descending with sort -rh. Excludes Xcode simulator runtimes by default (configurable).

Network-Tools.description: Flushes DNS cache, renews DHCP lease, removes stale network prefs, sets Cloudflare + Google DNS on all active interfaces.
Network-Tools.long_description: DNS flush: dscacheutil -flushcache + sudo killall -HUP mDNSResponder. DHCP: ipconfig set <interface> DHCP. Stale prefs: removes /Library/Preferences/SystemConfiguration/*.plist (network preferences, not entire SystemConfiguration). DNS set via networksetup -setdnsservers <interface> 1.1.1.1 8.8.8.8 for all active interfaces. Includes undo restoring DHCP auto DNS.

Network-Tools.description: Lists all saved WiFi SSIDs and retrieves stored passwords from the macOS Keychain. Each lookup triggers a system authentication prompt.
Network-Tools.long_description: networksetup -listpreferredwirelessnetworks <interface> to enumerate SSIDs. For each SSID: security find-generic-password -D 'AirPort network password' -a <SSID> -w. macOS shows a system dialog for each lookup requiring the user's login password or Touch ID. Passwords are printed to stdout only — never written to a file.

Network-Tools.description: Scans localhost for open TCP/UDP ports via nmap (if installed) or nc fallback. Maps each port to its process via lsof -i.
Network-Tools.long_description: If nmap installed: nmap -sV -p 1-65535 localhost (full scan with service detection). If not: manual loop using nc -z -w 1 for top 50 common ports. lsof -i -P -n | grep LISTEN resolves process ownership. Distinguishes privileged ports (<1024) from application ports. Output table: Port | Protocol | State | Service | Process | PID.

Security-Privacy.description: Disables macOS telemetry, Safari suggestions, Spotlight Siri data sharing, and analytics sharing. Optionally blocks Apple analytics domains in hosts file.
Security-Privacy.long_description: defaults write commands: com.apple.Safari UniversalSearchEnabled=0, SuppressSearchSuggestions=1; com.apple.assistant.support 'Search Queries Data Sharing Status'=2; /Library/Application Support/CrashReporter/DiagnosticMessagesHistory.plist AutoSubmit=false. launchctl unload com.apple.SubmitDiagInfo. Optional hosts append for ~20 Apple analytics endpoints. Restore option re-enables all.

Security-Privacy.description: Manages the macOS Application Firewall: enable/disable, stealth mode, list rules, add/remove app rules.
Security-Privacy.long_description: Uses /usr/libexec/ApplicationFirewall/socketfilterfw. Options: --setglobalstate on/off (enable firewall), --setstealthmode on (block ICMP/ping), --listapps (show all rules), --add /path/to/app + --blockapp/--unblockapp, --getglobalstate (status check). Prints final ruleset after each change for confirmation.

Security-Privacy.description: Reports Gatekeeper and SIP status. Lists quarantined files via xattr. Shows recently added Launch Agents and Daemons.
Security-Privacy.long_description: spctl --status (Gatekeeper assess state). csrutil status (SIP: enabled/disabled). xattr -r -l / 2>/dev/null | grep com.apple.quarantine (quarantined items, may be slow). ls -lt ~/Library/LaunchAgents /Library/LaunchAgents /Library/LaunchDaemons | head -20 (recently added launch items). Flags any Launch Agent added in last 7 days.

Utility-Scripts.description: Watches ~/Desktop (or configured Screenshots folder) and auto-renames new screenshots with date-time instead of default 'Screenshot YYYY-MM-DD at HH.MM.SS' format.
Utility-Scripts.long_description: Uses fswatch (brew install fswatch) to watch the screenshots directory. On new file matching 'Screenshot *.png', renames it to YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_screenshot.png. Configurable: target folder, output naming pattern, move to ~/Pictures/Screenshots archive. Runs as a background daemon.

System-Diagnostics.description: Dashboard from lscpu, free -h, df -h, lsblk, lspci, hostnamectl, and uptime in a clean formatted terminal report.
System-Diagnostics.long_description: Sections: OS (hostnamectl), Kernel (uname -r), CPU (lscpu: model, cores, threads, MHz), RAM (free -h: total/used/free/available), Disk (df -h filtered to physical mounts + lsblk tree view), GPU (lspci | grep -i vga/3d/display), Network (ip addr show active interfaces), Uptime (uptime -p).

System-Diagnostics.description: Full hardware inventory via dmidecode, lshw, and inxi. Saves timestamped report to ~/hardware_report_YYYYMMDD.txt.
System-Diagnostics.long_description: dmidecode -t 0,1,2,4,17 (BIOS, system, baseboard, processor, memory devices). lshw -short (full device tree in short format). inxi -Fxxxz (if installed: full system info with -z to hide private data). All output combined and saved to ~/hardware_report_$(date +%Y%m%d).txt. Installs inxi if missing and user consents.

System-Diagnostics.description: Live 5-second refresh CPU temperature monitor from /sys/class/thermal and lm-sensors. Color-coded warnings at 80°C and 90°C.
System-Diagnostics.long_description: Reads /sys/class/thermal/thermal_zone*/temp (divide by 1000 for Celsius). Also runs sensors (lm-sensors) if installed for per-core and per-package readings. Refresh: watch -n 5 equivalent via loop + clear. Color: normal=green, >80°C=yellow, >90°C=red (ANSI escape codes). CTRL+C to exit.

Maintenance-Cleaning.description: Multi-stage deep clean: apt cache, ~/.cache, /tmp, systemd journal, thumbnail cache, and redundant snap revisions. Reports reclaimed space at each step.
Maintenance-Cleaning.long_description: Steps with reclaimed space reporting: apt autoremove -y + apt clean (package cache), rm -rf ~/.cache/* (user cache, excludes ~/.cache/mozilla and active browser sessions), find /tmp /var/tmp -atime +3 -delete (temp files unused 3+ days), journalctl --vacuum-size=100M (journal trim), rm -rf ~/.cache/thumbnails/* (thumbnails), snap list --all + remove old revisions (keeps 2 latest). Total space summary at end.

Maintenance-Cleaning.description: Cleans system logs older than 7 days: journalctl vacuum, rotated .gz and .1 log files in /var/log. Does not touch active log files.
Maintenance-Cleaning.long_description: journalctl --vacuum-time=7d (removes journal entries >7 days). find /var/log -name '*.gz' -o -name '*.1' -o -name '*.old' -mtime +7 -delete (rotated archived logs). Reports disk used by /var/log before and after. Excludes: /var/log/journal (handled by journalctl), /var/log/auth.log (security audit trail), /var/log/syslog (current).

Maintenance-Cleaning.description: Removes orphaned packages via deborphan, unused older kernels (keeps current + 1), and broken symlinks in /usr/local.
Maintenance-Cleaning.long_description: deborphan (auto-installs if missing) identifies packages with no reverse dependencies. apt purge on confirmed list with user review step. Kernel cleanup: dpkg --list 'linux-image-*' identifies all installed kernels; uname -r identifies current; removes all others except the one immediately prior. find /usr/local -xtype l to list broken symlinks (listed for review, not auto-removed).

Network-Tools.description: Flushes systemd-resolved DNS, restarts network interface, sets Google/Cloudflare DNS in /etc/resolv.conf, runs latency test.
Network-Tools.long_description: resolvectl flush-caches (systemd-resolved) or /etc/init.d/dns-clean flush (legacy). ip link set <iface> down; sleep 2; ip link set <iface> up (restarts active interface). Writes 'nameserver 1.1.1.1' and 'nameserver 8.8.8.8' to /etc/resolv.conf (backs up original first). Ping test to 4 targets post-optimization. Restore option uses backed-up resolv.conf.

Network-Tools.description: Uses ss -tulnp to list all listening ports with owning processes. Optional nmap scan for service version detection.
Network-Tools.long_description: ss -tulnp (TCP+UDP, listening only, numeric ports, process info). Parses output into clean table: Port | Protocol | State | Process | PID. System ports (<1024) labeled separately from application ports. If nmap installed: optional nmap -sV localhost for service version detection. Highlights unusual ports that don't match known service names.

Network-Tools.description: Real-time bandwidth monitor. Uses nethogs, iftop, or vnstat in preference order. Falls back to /proc/net/dev polling.
Network-Tools.long_description: Detection order: command -v nethogs (per-process RX/TX, best), command -v iftop (per-connection), command -v vnstat (historical stats). If none: reads /proc/net/dev every 1 second, calculates delta RX/TX bytes, converts to KB/s or MB/s per interface. Offers to install nethogs via apt if not present. All tools run interactively (full terminal takeover).

Security-Privacy.description: Disables and masks whoopsie, apport, avahi-daemon, and cups. Appends ad/tracker domain blocks to /etc/hosts.
Security-Privacy.long_description: systemctl disable --now whoopsie apport avahi-daemon cups (if cups not required). systemctl mask whoopsie apport (prevents re-enable). Backs up /etc/hosts to /etc/hosts.bak. Appends ~100 ad/tracker domains sourced from StevenBlack hosts list. Restore option: systemctl unmask + start stopped services, restore /etc/hosts from backup.

Security-Privacy.description: Configures UFW with secure defaults: deny all incoming, allow outgoing, SSH from local subnet only, logging on.
Security-Privacy.long_description: ufw reset (clean state). ufw default deny incoming. ufw default allow outgoing. ufw allow from 192.168.0.0/24 to any port 22 (SSH local subnet only). ufw limit 22/tcp (rate-limit SSH for brute-force protection). ufw logging on. ufw enable. ufw status verbose (print final ruleset). Installs ufw via apt if not present.

Security-Privacy.description: Installs rkhunter and chkrootkit if missing, runs both scanners, combines output into a timestamped report at ~/security_scan_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.txt.
Security-Privacy.long_description: apt install rkhunter chkrootkit -y (if missing). rkhunter --update + rkhunter --check --skip-keypress --report-warnings-only. chkrootkit -q (quiet mode, shows only findings). Combines both outputs into a single report file. grep -E 'WARNING|INFECTED|FOUND' on the report and prints highlights to terminal in red. Report path printed at end.

Utility-Scripts.description: Interactive rsync backup: prompts for source and destination, creates a timestamped backup folder, shows live progress and total size on completion.
Utility-Scripts.long_description: rsync -avh --progress --stats <source> <dest>/BACKUP_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)/. --exclude='.git' --exclude='node_modules' --exclude='*.tmp'. Prints transfer speed, total files, and total size from rsync --stats output. Logs rsync output to backup_YYYYMMDD.log alongside backup. Compares source and dest file counts as verification step.
